text: Welcome to the Central Instrumentation Facility INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY JAMMU PALOURA CAMPUS, J&K, INDIA INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY JAMMU PALOURA CAMPUS, OPPOSITE TO JANIPUR POLICE STATION JAMMU 181121, J&K, INDIA Central Instrumentation Facility at a glance Enabling Cutting Edge Research….. Central Instrumentation Facility (SAPTARSHI), 2022-2023 Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF) named SAPTARSHI at IIT Jammu houses a wide array of high-end instruments for pushing the boundaries of research in science and technology to a higher level. The SAPTARSHI lab is a conglomeration of seven labs, namely; Microscopy Lab, Spectroscopy Lab, X-ray Based Characterization Lab, optics and chromatography lab, Nanofabrication and Physical Property Characterization lab (NPPCL), Thermal Analysis Lab (TAL), and Water Quality Testing Lab (WQTL). This is the heart of the institution, which helps the faculties, research scholars, and students to carry out globally competitive research in basic, applied, and medical sciences and publish their research findings in peer-reviewed high-impact factor journals for a sustainable society. The Center also hopes to expand the facilities each year, making it a core facility in the country. By realizing CIF (SAPTARSHI), we expect a prominent hub for pioneering and collaborative analytical research in our country. This facility is extensively used by internal doctoral students and faculty members and extended to external organizations, academic institutions as well as the industry in the country on a chargeable basis. This facility is also used for organizing short-term courses/workshops on the use and application of various instruments and analytical techniques. The CIF (SAPTARSHI) is expected to self-sustain by revenue generation for the upkeep and maintenance of the instruments. Flow chart for booking……
1. Go to the booking portal: “BOOKING@SAPTARSHI” on IIT Jammu web page.
Follow the below link: https://www.iitjammu.ac.in/cif/booking-saptarshi
2. Register yourself by putting appropriate credentials.
3. You will get a log in credentials in your registered email.
4. Please book the instrument as per your requirement and upload the requisition form
(duly signed by your PI) also.
5. The concerned in charge will approve your booking (It will take few days).
6. After approval in your portal you will get a payment gateway.
7. Follow the instruction for the payment section accordingly.
8. Only after successful payment (verified by the account section), your booking will be
confirmed.
N.B.:
1. You can check your sample analysis date in the “remarks section” in your log in portal.
The nearest date of analysis is allotted as per the sample rush at that time.
2. In the mean time you can send your sample as per your convenience through courier/by
post/in person to the mentioned CIF address as below:
Officer In-Charge, CIF, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, Paloura campus, Opposite
to Janipur Police Station, Paloura, Jammu. Jammu and Kashmir, Pincode-181121.
3. Please mention the concerned instrument on envelop. For example “ sample for
XPS analysis…”.

# Page Title:  Accounts Associate – I3C IIT Jammu

## Accounts Associate

### Last Date of Application: July 10, 2026

## Position Details

##### Location

**IIT Jammu Campus, Jagti, Jammu**

##### Key Responsibilities

The selected candidate will be responsible for supporting the day-to-day accounts and finance operations, including:

- Processing and maintaining accounting records and vouchers.
- Verification and compilation of invoices, bills, and supporting documents.
- Assisting in payment processing and banking-related activities.
- Maintaining records related to GST, TDS, and other statutory compliances.
- Reconciliation of accounts, bank statements, and financial records.
- Supporting internal and external audits.
- Maintaining project-wise and program-wise financial documentation.
- Assisting in preparation of financial reports and MIS.
- Any other accounts, finance, or administrative work assigned from time to time.

##### Essential Qualifications

- Bachelor’s degree in Commerce (B.Com) / Finance / Accounting or equivalent from a recognized institution.

##### Desirable Qualifications

- M.Com, MBA (Finance), CA Inter, CMA Inter, or equivalent qualifications.
- Knowledge of accounting software such as Tally, Busy, Zoho Books, or similar ERP systems.
- Working knowledge of GST, TDS, PF, ESI, and statutory compliance.
- Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and other Office applications.

##### Experience

- Minimum 1 to 3 years of relevant experience in accounts, finance, audit, taxation, or related functions.
- Experience in educational institutions, incubators, startups, government projects, NGOs, or Section 8 companies will be preferred.

##### Desired Competencies

- Attention to detail and accuracy.
- Strong ethical standards and integrity.
- Ability to maintain confidentiality of financial information.
- Good communication and documentation skills.
- Ability to work independently as well as in a team environment.
- Willingness to learn and adapt to evolving organizational requirements.

##### Consolidated Remuneration

**₹25,000 – ₹30,000 per month**
(Commensurate with qualifications and experience)

##### Nature of Appointment

Full-time contractual position, initially for **one year**, extendable based on performance and organizational requirements.

##### How to Apply

Interested candidates may submit their application at:

**https://forms.gle/FEV85eG3nRrLvFjK9**

##### Note

- Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for the further selection process.
- I3C reserves the right to accept or reject any application without assigning any reason and to modify the number of positions or terms of engagement as deemed necessary.

# Page Title: Assistant Incubation Manager – I3C IIT Jammu

## Assistant Incubation Manager

Apply Now

## About the Job

**Company Description**

The Institute Incubation and Innovation Council (I3C) is the startup incubator of IIT Jammu, operating as a Section-8, not-for-profit organization fully owned by the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. I3C is committed to fostering innovation and entrepreneurship by providing support, mentorship, and resources to aspiring entrepreneurs. Its mission is to empower India’s future business leaders while promoting innovation and economic growth in the Jammu & Kashmir region and beyond. The council plays a vital role in building a robust startup ecosystem with a long-term positive impact.

**Role Description**

This is a full-time, on-site role located in Jammu for an Assistant Program Manager. The Assistant Program Manager will manage day-to-day operations, coordinate with startups, and assist in organizing events and workshops. Responsibilities include overseeing program activities, building and maintaining relationships with stakeholders, and assisting with incubator operations. The individual will also provide administrative support, monitor project progress, and report key metrics to ensure the successful functioning of the incubation centre.

**Qualifications**

- Strong organizational and project management skills, including the ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
- Proficiency in written and verbal communication, report writing, and preparing presentations.
- Basic understanding of startup ecosystems, entrepreneurship, and innovation processes.
- Experience in stakeholder engagement, networking, and relationship management.
- Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities.
- Proficiency in the use of productivity tools such as MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
- A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business Administration, Management, or a related field is preferred.
- Familiarity with the technology or startup ecosystem in Jammu & Kashmir is a plus.
- Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a dynamic work environment.

**Ideal Candidate Profile**

- 2–5 years of experience in operations / administration / startup ecosystem / Incubators & Accelerator
- Highly organized, detail-oriented, and proactive
- Strong communication and coordination skills

# Page Title:  Craft Documentation & Product Development Designer – I3C IIT Jammu

## Craft Documentation & Product Development Designer

under the project:

“Development of Bakarwal Tribal Craft Embroidery Cooperative Society – A Pilot Youth-Led Livelihood and Cultural Preservation Initiative”

implemented in collaboration with the Department of Tribal Affairs, Government of Jammu & Kashmir.

### Last Date of Application: June 20, 2026

## About the Project

The project aims to strengthen sustainable livelihoods of Bakarwal women and youth through craft documentation, embroidery skill enhancement, product development, and cooperative institution building rooted in indigenous knowledge systems and cultural preservation.

The selected candidate will work closely with Bakarwal artisan communities in Kashmir and Samba districts, engaging in field-based design intervention, embroidery documentation, product prototyping, and collaborative development processes.

## Position Details

Duration: Initially for **6 Months & extendable to 24 months**

Location
Field-based engagement across:

- Samba District, Jammu & Kashmir

Remuneration

- **Rs. 30,000/- per month**
- Basic accommodation in artisan/community homes will be provided during field engagement.

## Key Areas of Work

- Documentation of Bakarwal embroidery traditions, motifs, stories, and craft processes;
- Collaborative product development with women artisans and youth;
- Development of contemporary yet culturally rooted craft products;
- Assistance in design systems, sampling, and prototype development;
- Working with artisan groups on quality improvement and product refinement;
- Support in visual documentation and process archiving;
- Coordination with project partners and community youth leaders.

## Project Community & Skills

##### Community

Bakarwal Tribal Community

##### Craft Focus

Traditional Embroidery & Textile-based Craft Practices

## Desired Experience

Applicants should preferably have experience in:

- Craft documentation and product development work with textile/craft clusters (approximately 2 months to 2 years);
- Working with design houses and/or B2B sourcing environments;
- Field-based artisan engagement and community-led design processes.

## Qualification

Applicants should possess:

- Master’s Degree in Textiles and/or Fashion Design from a reputed institution;

**OR**

- Bachelor’s Degree in a relevant design discipline with minimum 2 years of work experience.

## Application Process

Interested applicants may email the following:

1. Updated CV
2. Motivation Letter
3. A brief process flow/note explaining how they would approach and achieve the project deliverables within 6 months

Email Applications To: info@i3c-iitjammu.in

Subject Line:

**Application – Craft Documentation & Product Development Designer**

Women candidates and applicants with prior experience working in community-led craft ecosystems are especially encouraged to apply.

# Page Title: Catalyst 30 – I3C IIT Jammu

## Catalyst 30

(Under SIDBI Seed Equity Support Scheme)

Apply Now

## About the Program

Catalyst 30 is a targeted investment-readiness and seed funding program launched by I3C – IIT Jammu, with support from SIDBI, to empower early-stage, revenue-generating startups. The program provides structured mentorship, financial planning support, and equity funding of up to ₹30 lakhs to help founders strengthen their business models, improve investor readiness, and scale sustainably.

## Objective

1. **Enable early-stage startups** to become investment-ready through expert-led mentoring and financial planning.
2. **Provide equity-based seed funding** of up to ₹30 lakhs to help startups achieve key growth milestones.
3. **Facilitate investor access** and support scalable, revenue-generating startups on their path to institutional funding.

## Key Component

### Mentorship

1-1 mentoring with sector experts, investors, and Industry Experts

### Post-Funding Support

Ongoing guidance on fund utilization, compliance, and follow-on fundraising

### Networking Events

With potential investors, banks, corporate partners, and fellow entrepreneurs.

## Criteria

1. Registered and recognized by DPIIT
2. Generating consistent revenue for at least 6 months
3. Seeking to raise between ₹15 to ₹30 lakhs for scaling operations

## Cohort

### Contact us

akash_sharma@i3c-iitjammu.in / +91-8825099833

Apply Now

## FAQs

What is Catalyst 30?

Catalyst 30 is an investment-readiness and seed funding program launched by I3C – IIT Jammu with support from SIDBI. It provides structured mentorship, financial planning support, and equity-based seed funding of up to ₹30 lakhs for early-stage, revenue-generating startups.

Who can apply for Catalyst 30?

Startups that are:

- Registered and recognized by DPIIT
- Generating consistent revenue for at least 6 months
- Looking to raise ₹15–₹30 lakhs for scaling operations

What kind of funding is offered?

Selected startups can receive equity-based seed funding of up to ₹30 lakhs under the SIDBI Seed Equity Support Scheme.

What support does the program provide apart from funding?

- **1:1 mentorship** with sector experts, investors, and industry leaders
- **Financial planning support** for fund utilization and compliance
- **Networking opportunities** with VCs, banks, and corporate partners
- **Post-funding support** for scaling and follow-on fundraising

Is there a fee to apply for the program?

No. There is no fee to apply or participate in Catalyst 30.

How long is the program?

The program runs in multiple phases, including mentoring, funding support, and post-investment guidance. Timelines will be shared with shortlisted startups.

What sectors are eligible?

Catalyst 30 is sector-agnostic and open to all startups that meet the eligibility criteria, with a focus on scalable, revenue-generating ventures.

Will I need to give equity?

Yes, Catalyst 30 funding is equity/SAFE-based, not debt. The exact terms will be discussed during the funding process.

How do I apply?

Applications can be submitted online through the provided application link. Shortlisted startups will be contacted for further evaluation rounds.

What is the selection process?

Applications will be evaluated based on:

- Innovation and scalability of the business model
- Revenue traction and market potential
- Strength of the founding team
- Alignment with investor readiness goals

When will funding be disbursed?

Funding will be disbursed after the due diligence process and signing of the investment agreements.

Who are the mentors and investors involved?

Catalyst 30 brings in a curated pool of mentors from industry, academia, and the investment ecosystem, along with access to SIDBI and I3C’s investor network.

# Page Title: Central Workshop

## Message from OIC-Central Workshop

At the Central Workshop-IIT JAMMU, we envision ourselves as the heart of practical knowledge and academic excellence. We are committed to providing students with a dynamic and immersive learning experience that transcends traditional education. Our goal is to nurture not only their theoretical understanding but also their hands-on skills, making them well-rounded professionals prepared to conquer the challenges of the real world. Our mission is to equip them with the knowledge and practical skills that are essential for their success both in academia and in the industry. Collaboration with local industries is at the core of our vision. We recognize the tremendous value that can be derived from such partnerships. Central Workshop is not just a place for learning; it's a strategic partner in your quest for innovation and growth. We encourage local industries to explore the possibilities of collaboration with us. Together, we can drive innovation, stimulate economic growth, and cultivate the talent of the future. Together, we can create a brighter future.

Mr. Arun Kumar
Asst. Workshop Officer

Rules and Regulations of Central Workshop - IITJMU
1. Don't wear loose or flammable/silky clothes in the workshop.
2. Never walk bare footed (or with slippers) inside the workshop, wear rubber-soled fully
covered shoes
3. Don't wear half-pant or Capri in the workshop.
4. Never run nor play inside the workshop or any other laboratory.
5. Never operate any machine without proper training and supervision know
how to operate it and the instructor is not available to guide you at the moment.
6. Never touch moving parts, belts or rotating tools, etc.
7. Don't use defective tools i.e, you must inspect everything before starting any task.
8. Long hair must be tied properly.
9. Rings, necklaces (anything that can be caught in rotating machinery parts) must be
removed before operating the machine.
10. Always wear eye protection and lab coats when using power operated hand or machine
tools or while performing physical tasks.
11. Don't touch any live wire inside the workshop.
12. In case of fire, the electric supply should be disconnected and you must know the
emergency button.
13. Don't bring eatables into the workshop. Never indulge in reckless behaviour in the
workshop.
14. Don't keep any tooling or parts close to the edge of the workbench or table, from where it
may fall and injure someone.
15. Don't lean or rest against any machine.
16. Don't talk to others while they are operating a machine. Similarly concentrate on your work
and don't talk unnecessary while operating machine.
17. Don't leave tools or work parts on the table of the machine bed even if machine is not in
the operation. You must clean the area used by you to prevent injury to others through
slipping/falling.
18. Get first aid immediately for any injury. You must always be aware of the location of the
first aid box, fire extinguishers etc. Similarly, immediately inform the workshop instructor if
anyone else gets injured.
19. All equipment in the workshop is designed to cut through metal so any such equipment
can cut through human body parts very easily if people are careless and violate safety
regulations. Hence, students MUST follow all safety rules strictly in order to be allowed to
use the workshop. If you observe any violation of safety rules that is happening or about
to happen, you MUST take immediate action to prevent it and inform the workshop
supervisor.

### About

**The Central Workshop at IIT Jammu:**

**Nurturing Precision Engineering and Innovation**

Central Workshop at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu is the place where we work together to attain excellence and create a unique centralized facility for manufacturing by providing latest technology under one roof.  This central facility is available for designing and manufacturing of products to the faculty, staff, and students of IIT Jammu as well as for external agencies. Here, various interesting projects have been developed, and some of which have been patented. The CWS is equipped with latest manufacturing equipment, and is expanded in 4500 sq. feet area which is having two sections viz. machining and fabrication section. Machining section is equipped with wire EDM, abrasive water jet machine, CNC & conventional machines, grinders, drill machines, 3D printers and vision measuring system. Fabrication section contains carpentry, welding, sheet metal, FRP, injection moulding and other fitting facilities. Our enthusiastic educated technical team is always ready to conquer the challenges appear before them.

**Our Mission:**

At the Central Workshop, our mission is to provide a dynamic environment for the engineering community to explore, create, and innovate. We are dedicated to empower students with practical skills, enabling ground breaking research endeavours, and offering invaluable support for projects and coursework.

**What We Offer:**

- **State-of-the-Art Facilities**: Our workshop is equipped with a
wide array of advanced machinery and tools, ensuring that students and faculty
have access to the best resources for their projects.
- **Safety First**: We prioritize safety above all else. Rigorous safety training and adherence to safety protocols are integral to our operations, which ensure a secure and productive environment.
- **Collaborative Learning**: The Central Workshop is a space for collaboration and interdisciplinary learning. We encourage students and researchers to work together, share knowledge, and push the boundaries of innovation.
- **Expert Guidance**: Our dedicated team of experienced and well educated staff is here to provide technical guidance, support, and training to help you achieve your goals.

**Summer Internship Program at Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu

Duration: 4-6 Weeks (Annually)**

The Central Workshop at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu is proud to announce that we offer a Summer Internship Program annually during the months of June and July for which the registration process starts in the month of May. This program has marked a significant milestone in our commitment to nurturing young talent and fostering hands-on learning experiences.

**Objective**: The primary goal of the Summer Internship Program is to provide a platform for aspiring engineering students and researchers to gain practical insights and technical skills in a real-world workshop environment. It aims to bridge the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical application while equipping interns with valuable engineering and fabrication expertise.

**Internship Highlights:** During this internship program, we will have the privilege of hosting and training enthusiastic interns from diverse engineering backgrounds. The internship program feature following highlights:

- **Hands-On Experience:** Interns will have the opportunity to work directly with state-of-the-art equipment and machinery available in the Central Workshop. They will gain the practical experience in machining, fabrication, 3D printing, and many more.
.
- **Project Work:** Each intern will be assigned a project tailored to their field of interest and expertise. These projects range from designing and prototyping engineering components and setups.
- **Guidance from Experts:** Our experienced workshop team provides mentorship and guidance throughout the internship, ensuring that interns receive the necessary technical support to excel in their projects.
- **Ex-Interns' Achievements** Throughout the program, our interns demonstrated exceptional dedication and creativity. They successfully completed their assigned projects and showcased their accomplishments in a final presentation to the faculty, workshop staff and fellow interns. These projects not only added value to their academic and professional growth but also contributed to the workshop's ongoing research and development efforts.

**Program Highlights:**

- Earn a prestigious certification from IIT Jammu, with the option of an exclusive campus immersion.
- Learn through Virtual Instructor-Led Training (VILT) with IIT Jammu faculty and SkillUp Online mentors.
- Gain industry-recognized credentials including IBM certifications and micro-credentials from leading tech partners.
- Experience hands-on labs, guided projects, and real-world scenarios designed to strengthen your job readiness.
- Receive personalized mentoring, continuous assessments, and academic feedback to support your progress.
- Access all content, recordings, and evaluations through SkillUp’s advanced learning platform till the issuance of the Certificate.

**Learning Outcomes:**

By completing this program, you will:

- Strengthen your foundation in mathematics, statistics, and algorithms for AI and ML.
- Develop advanced Python programming skills for data analysis and AI application development.
- Use Pandas, NumPy, and SciPy to clean, manipulate, and visualize structured and unstructured data.
- Write optimized SQL queries to manage relational databases.
- Build interactive dashboards and visual reports using Tableau and IBM Cognos.

- Explore deep learning techniques for computer vision, NLP, and generative AI use cases.
- Work with Azure AI services to deploy cloud-native AI applications.
- Follow ethical AI guidelines and ensure data privacy in your projects.
- Showcase your learning through an industry-oriented capstone project.

**Governing Committee:**

A dedicated governing committee composed of IIT Jammu faculty and industry experts ensures that this program meets IIT Jammu’s academic standards and maintains real-world industry relevance.

**Eligibility Criteria:**

- Preferred Profile: Working professionals and tech-oriented career switchers keen to build a professional base in AI and Data Science.

- Graduates eager to start a career in AI, data science, or analytics.
- IT professionals looking to transition into AI and machine learning roles.
- Developers and project managers who want to expand their skillset into data-driven decision-making.
- Technology students aim to enhance their academic learning with hands-on AI exposure.

### About us

Central Instrumentation Facility (CIF) named SAPTARSHI at IIT Jammu houses a wide array of high-end instruments for pushing the boundaries of research in science and technology to a higher level. The SAPTARSHI lab is a conglomeration of seven labs, namely; Microscopy Lab, Spectroscopy Lab, X-ray Based Characterization Lab, optics and chromatography lab, Nanofabrication and Physical Property Characterization lab (NPPCL), Thermal Analysis Lab (TAL), and Water Quality Testing Lab (WQTL). This is the heart of the institution, which helps the faculties, research scholars, and students to carry out globally competitive research in basic, applied, and medical sciences and publish their research findings in peer-reviewed high-impact factor journals for a sustainable society.

The Central Instrumentation Facility (Saptarshi Labs) of IIT Jammu at Paloura campus is well equipped with highly sophisticated state of the art instruments, in order to enable researchers to peep into the unexplored domains of basic sciences and engineering. # Page Title: IIT Jammu and CSIR-IIIM join forces to nurture startups and drive regional innovation – I3C IIT Jammu

In a major step towards strengthening the innovation and entrepreneurship ecosystem in Jammu and Kashmir, the **Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu** and the **CSIR–Indian Institute of Integrative Medicine (CSIR-IIIM), Jammu**, have signed a **Tripartite Memorandum of Understanding (MoU)** with three promising start-ups — **Biogreen Projects, Evlogia Eco Care, and Crop Domain** — on **3rd August 2025**.

This collaboration marks a significant milestone in advancing the vision of **Startup India** and **Stand-Up India**, inspired by the leadership of **Hon’ble Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi** and **Hon’ble Union Minister Dr. Jitendra Singh**, Vice President, CSIR.

The partnership aims to **bridge cutting-edge research with societal impact**, fostering an environment where science and entrepreneurship work hand in hand. Speaking on the occasion, **Prof. Manoj Singh Gaur**, Director, IIT Jammu, remarked,

“This tripartite alliance exemplifies IIT Jammu’s commitment to bridging cutting-edge research with societal impact.”

Echoing this sentiment, **Dr. Zabeer Ahmed**, Director, CSIR-IIIM, stated,

“This initiative will strengthen the innovation and startup ecosystem and enable faster translation of scientific and engineering research into commercially viable technologies.”

The collaboration focuses on key areas such as:

- **Joint R&D and product development**
- **Incubation, mentorship, and lab-to-market acceleration**
- **Skill-building, workshops, and employment generation**
- **Commercial scale-up in biotechnology, sustainability, and agri-innovation**

**Startup Spotlight:**

- **Biogreen Projects:** 100% compostable bioplastics
- **Evlogia Eco Care:** Leafy straws and biodegradable tableware
- **Crop Domain:** Climate-smart agri-bio solutions

The event also featured insights from **Dr. Saurabh Saran**, Principal Scientist, CSIR-IIIM-TBI, and **Dr. Arvind**, CEO, I3C, IIT Jammu, who emphasized the collaborative vision of building a thriving ecosystem that connects research, innovation, and entrepreneurship.

Together, IIT Jammu, CSIR-IIIM, and their partner start-ups are committed to **transforming Jammu into a hub of innovation**, fostering sustainable technologies that address real-world challenges and create inclusive growth opportunities for the region.

# Page Title: IIT Jammu and PHN Technology Join Hands to Establish Centre of Excellence in Robotics, AI, and IoT – I3C IIT Jammu

The **Institute Incubation and Innovation Council (I3C)** at the **Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu** has partnered with **PHN Technology Pvt. Ltd.** to establish a **Centre of Excellence (CoE)** in **Robotics, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and the Internet of Things (IoT)** at the IIT Jammu Jagti campus.

As part of this collaboration, PHN Technology has pledged an investment of **₹50 lakhs** to fully fund the establishment of the CoE, including laboratory infrastructure and equipment procurement. The Centre is expected to become fully operational within the next **two months**, positioning IIT Jammu as a leading hub for innovation and research in Robotics and AI.

The CoE aims to create a vibrant ecosystem where **students, faculty members, and industry experts** collaborate on cutting-edge robotics and AI projects. It will facilitate workshops, research initiatives, and active knowledge exchange between academia and industry, fostering a culture of **innovation, experimentation, and applied learning**.

Aligned with IIT Jammu’s mission to promote technology-driven growth and entrepreneurship, the Centre will play a pivotal role in **advancing the robotics, AI, and IoT ecosystem** in the **Jammu and Kashmir region**. By offering access to state-of-the-art facilities and industry mentorship, the CoE will enable students to gain **hands-on experience and practical insights** into these transformative technologies.

The establishment of this Centre of Excellence marks a significant milestone in IIT Jammu’s efforts to **drive technological innovation, attract investments, and build a skilled workforce** for the future. It is envisioned as a catalyst that will empower the next generation of innovators, researchers, and entrepreneurs to contribute meaningfully to India’s growing robotics and AI landscape.

Through this MoU, IIT Jammu aims to strengthen its ecosystem for applied research and experiential learning, while contributing to the national mission of fostering technological self-reliance.

This alliance underscores IIT Jammu’s continued commitment to building strong industry-academia linkages and empowering students and researchers to contribute meaningfully to the technological advancement of the nation.

**Together, IIT Jammu and PHN Technology aspire to push the frontiers of Robotics and AI — driving transformation through innovation, collaboration, and education.**

# Page Title: IIT Jammu Concludes 5-Day Faculty Development Program on Innovation & Entrepreneurship – I3C IIT Jammu

The **Institute Incubation and Innovation Council (I3C)** at the **Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu** successfully concluded a transformative **5-day Faculty Development Program (FDP)** on **Innovation and Entrepreneurship**, organized in collaboration with the **All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE)** and the **Ministry of Education’s Innovation Cell (MoE’s IIC)**.

The program aimed to inspire a **mindset shift among educators**, empowering them to nurture and guide future innovators within their institutions. Over five days, the FDP brought together an exceptional cohort of faculty members, thought leaders, and startup enthusiasts to explore the foundations of entrepreneurial education and innovation-driven learning.

The sessions covered a range of key topics, including:

- **Fundamentals of Innovation and Entrepreneurship** – Understanding how ideas evolve into impactful ventures.
- **Design Thinking and Problem Solving** – Enabling educators to identify real-world challenges and guide students toward sustainable solutions.
- **Business Model Canvas and Go-to-Market Strategies** – Integrating entrepreneurial frameworks into academic curricula.
- **Financial Planning, Funding, and Scalability** – Exploring the essentials of venture creation and long-term sustainability.
- **Intellectual Property and Legal Frameworks** – Educating faculty on protecting and commercializing innovation.

The program fostered an environment of **active participation, collaboration, and knowledge exchange**, reinforcing the belief that entrepreneurship begins in the classroom.

IIT Jammu extends heartfelt gratitude to all the **eminent speakers, mentors, and facilitators** who shared their expertise and real-world insights, making the sessions engaging and impactful.

To all the participating faculty members — your enthusiasm, curiosity, and commitment were the driving forces behind this program’s success. As you carry these learnings forward, IIT Jammu and I3C look forward to seeing you inspire, innovate, and cultivate entrepreneurial thinking within your institutions.

This initiative reaffirms IIT Jammu’s commitment to **building a vibrant startup ecosystem**, supporting educators, students, and entrepreneurs in shaping the future of innovation and enterprise in India.

# Page Title: Mentoring Clinic – I3C IIT Jammu

## Mentoring Clinics

Guiding Ideas to Impact

Apply Now

## About the Program

Aspiring and early-stage entrepreneurs often struggle to access relevant, high-quality mentorship at the right time. Without the right guidance, many promising ideas fail to progress into viable ventures. At the Institute Incubation and Innovation Council (I3C) – IIT Jammu, we aim to bridge this gap through a structured mentoring platform that connects innovators to the right experts.

## Objective

The I3C Mentoring Clinic will act as a single-window platform for entrepreneurs to raise their queries and be matched with mentors who can provide targeted, actionable advice.

## Target Audience

1. Aspiring entrepreneurs (idea stage)
2. Early-stage founders/startups
3. Students with innovative projects
4. MSMEs seeking innovation guidance

## Process Flow

1. **Query Submission –** Entrepreneurs fill out a Google Form embedded on the I3C website, detailing their idea, current challenges, and the type of support needed.
2. **Internal Mapping –** The I3C team reviews the request and connects the entrepreneur with a relevant mentor from our pool of experts—faculty, industry leaders, alumni founders, and domain specialists.
3. **Mentoring Session –** Initial mentoring session(s) are scheduled (online or offline) based on mentor availability.
4. **Follow-up Support –** Additional sessions or resources are arranged as needed to track progress and provide continued guidance.

## Key Features

- The first 2 hours of mentoring are completely free for every entrepreneur.
- Post the free sessions, additional mentoring will be chargeable as mutually agreed between the mentor and the founder.
- Access to a diverse mentor pool across technology, business, legal, and funding domains.
- Hybrid mentoring options (in-person or online).
- Flexible scheduling based on mentor availability.

### Expected Outcomes

- Increased clarity in startup roadmaps and strategies.
- Higher rate of idea-to-market conversion.
- Strengthened startup ecosystem in J&K with quality mentor access.

Apply Now

## FAQs

Who can apply for mentoring?

Any aspiring entrepreneur, early-stage startup founder, student innovator, or MSME owner seeking guidance in business, technology, product development, or scaling can apply.

How do I apply?

Simply fill out the Google Form available on the I3C website, detailing your idea, stage of development, and specific challenges you are facing.

How are mentors assigned?

Once we receive your query, our team will review it and connect you with the most relevant mentor based on your domain, challenge, and requirements.

What is the cost of the mentoring sessions?

The first 2 hours of mentoring are completely free. Any additional sessions will be chargeable, with fees mutually decided between the mentor and the founder.

How often can I access the mentoring clinic?

You can submit a request anytime during the year. The frequency of mentoring depends on your needs, mentor availability, and progress from previous sessions.

Are sessions online or in person?

We offer both options—mentoring can be conducted either in-person at I3C or online, depending on mentor and mentee preference.

What is the duration of each session?

Session duration will be mutually decided between the mentor and the entrepreneur, depending on the scope of discussion.

What kind of mentors will I get access to?

Our mentor pool includes industry experts, seasoned entrepreneurs, IIT alumni, domain specialists, and faculty members experienced in guiding startups.

How will I know if my application is accepted?

Once we map your request to a mentor, you will receive an email confirmation with the mentor’s details and proposed time slots.

How frequently does the mentoring clinic operate?

The clinic is ongoing throughout the year, with mentor mapping and sessions scheduled as per request submissions and availability.

## Bio Green

Biogreen is an innovative startup focused on sustainable and eco-friendly solutions. It specializes in biodegradable products that help reduce environmental impact by offering sustainable alternatives to traditional plastic-based materials. The startup aims to promote green technology and create a positive environmental footprint through its range of products.

## Machfly Aerospace

SAP Aerospace is a deep-tech startup incubated at IIT Jammu, focused on developing compact, fuel-flexible gas turbine engines for UCAVs and loitering munitions. Their flagship product, RUDRA, features an indigenously built 1 kN turbojet engine capable of high-altitude and high-speed operations. The startup is equipped with advanced propulsion labs and testing facilities. Recognized by DRDO’s Dare to Dream 4.0 and Boeing BUILD 2.0, SAP Aerospace is emerging as a key innovator in India’s aerospace and defense sector.

## Sakar Robotics

Sakar Robotics is a Pune-based Indian startup founded in 2023, focused on developing AI-powered mobile robotics and machine vision solutions for sectors like construction, manufacturing, railways, and biotech. The company offers “3A” solutions — Automated, Affordable, and Agile — to address labor-intensive challenges. Their innovations include an indigenous wall plastering robot and AI-driven bedroll inspection systems for Indian Railways. Sakar Robotics is emerging as a key enabler of intelligent automation in India’s industrial landscape.

## Crop Domain

Crop Domain is an agritech startup focused on climate-smart, precision, and sustainable agriculture solutions. Their flagship product, microSANJEEVINI™ Decomposer, accelerates composting, improves soil fertility, and enhances carbon sequestration. With a composting period of just 7-10 days, their 100% organic solution helps reduce greenhouse gas emissions and promotes eco-friendly farming without the use of chemicals.

## Yasil Technologies

RIU (Reporting Intelligence Unit) is a Bengaluru-based Indian startup offering AI-powered ESG compliance and carbon accounting solutions. Founded in 2022, RIU helps businesses streamline ESG reporting, track real-time carbon data, and assess sustainability performance across their supply chains. Their platform supports global frameworks like GRI, CDP, and BRSR. RIU has worked with leading enterprises and was part of Brigade REAP’s 17th cohort.

## Evlogia Eco Care

Evlogia Eco Care focused on eco-friendly tableware and takeaway containers made from agricultural residue like rice bran and husk. Their products are 100% natural, compostable, and animal-edible, aiming to replace plastic and reduce environmental impact. With a strong export potential and co-operative production model, Evlogia is working towards large-scale sustainable manufacturing while generating rural employment​.

## Studio Agama

Studio Agama Pvt. Ltd., founded by conservation architect Rahul Bhola and incubated at I3C – IIT Jammu, is a heritage conservation startup specializing in the digitization, documentation, and restoration of endangered cultural sites. Using cutting-edge technologies like 3D mapping, LIDAR, and 3D printing, the firm offers innovative, sustainable solutions to preserve architectural heritage. Their work supports institutions like the ASI and J&K Tourism, blending traditional craftsmanship with modern conservation science.

## Karroh

Karroh Private Limited is an interdisciplinary solutions-driven company committed to advancing sustainable environmental and transportation systems. We offer innovative, cost-effective services in water and waste treatment, circular economy advisory, pollution monitoring, and smart environmental analytics. Our expertise includes smart sensors, compact treatment units, and AI-integrated dashboards for real-time environmental management. On the transportation side, we specialize in planning, traffic engineering, road safety, ITS, sustainable mobility, logistics, and inland water transport. With a strong focus on policy, regulatory support, and training, Karroh empowers cities and industries to adopt integrated, eco-friendly, and technology-enabled solutions that drive long-term environmental and urban resilience.
